query: We're evaluating Postgres vs DynamoDB for our user-events table. We expect 10M events/day and need to run complex aggregation queries.
expected_behavior: Presents the tradeoffs of both options in a comparison format (not just a preference)
Recommends writing an ADR regardless of the final choice
Factors in the aggregation query requirement as a concrete tradeoff criterion
Does not pick silently — states a recommendation and explains the 'Why'
Does not generate application code to implement the choice
